notes: match(list, pattern[, sep]): returns the 1-based index of the first element of list (split on sep, default space, same split rule as words()/first()) wildcard-matching pattern, or 0 if none match. Unlike words/first/rest, an empty (post-trim) list is NOT zero elements -- functions.c's matching loop is a do/while that always runs at least once, so it still checks a single empty-string token (match(,*) is 1, not 0). extract(list, first, len[, sep]): first/len are atoi()-truncated; first<1 or len<1 silently returns empty. Otherwise returns the len elements starting at the first one (1-based), clamped (not an error) if fewer than len elements remain. Critically NOT a slice of a full split rejoined -- functions.c's fun_extract only uses next_token (never split_token) to locate offsets, and next_token never mutates the buffer, so the result is a raw substring preserving every interior separator run verbatim, regardless of sep. Ordinary typed spaces never reveal this (general-argument-evaluator pre-compression collapses them first), but backslash-escaped spaces do: extract(a\ \ b\ \ \ c,1,3) keeps both interior multi-space runs untouched, unlike rest() which only preserves runs after the first collapsed boundary.
